name: Circular Route 13 of Mondim de Basto — Alvão Natural Park
name_original: Percurso circular 13 de Mondim de Basto — Parque Natural do Alvão
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mondim de Basto are characterized by varied terrain, including paths through the Alvão Natural Park and along the Tâmega Line Greenway. The region features a mix of river valleys, former railway lines, and elevated areas offering panoramic views. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse natural landscapes and connect local towns. Best touring cycling routes around Mondim De Basto The most popular touring cycling route is Circular Route 13 of Mondim de Basto — Alvão Natural Park, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Celorico de Basto Ecotrail – Tâmega Line Greenway loop from Mondim de Basto, a difficult 37.5 miles (60.3 km) path. This route follows a scenic greenway, offering views along the former railway line. Local touring cyclists also love the Panoramic view from the hairpin loop from Mondim de Basto, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) trail leading through hilly terrain with scenic overlooks, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Mondim de Basto is defined by natural park landscapes, greenways, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for moderate and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Mondim de Basto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondim de Basto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mondim de Basto?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Mondim de Basto, offering a variety of experiences. These include paths through the Alvão Natural Park and along the Tâmega Line Greenway.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mondim de Basto?
A: While many routes in Mondim de Basto are classified as moderate or difficult due to the varied terrain, there are options that cater to different ability levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32160838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Climb or Descent Pathway – Celorico de Basto Ecotrail loop from Celorico de Basto&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that covers about 12.3 miles (19.7 km).

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Mondim de Basto area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292244875/circular-route-13-of-mondim-de-basto-alvao-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route 13 of Mondim de Basto — Alvão Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding ride. This route is 30.4 miles (48.9 km) long with significant elevation changes, exploring the natural park's landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Mondim de Basto?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celorico de Basto Ecotrail – Tâmega Line Greenway loop from Mondim de Basto&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) loop following a scenic greenway.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Mondim de Basto?
A: Mondim de Basto's touring cycling routes offer diverse scenery, including river valleys, sections of the former Tâmega railway line, and elevated areas with panoramic views. You'll also pass through parts of the Alvão Natural Park, known for its natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4518272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de Baúlhe Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/328221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermelo Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tâmega Line Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, offering a unique cycling experience along a repurposed railway line.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints in Mondim de Basto?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its hilly terrain, providing numerous opportunities for scenic overlooks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23741683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view from the hairpin loop from Mondim de Basto&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase these vistas, offering stunning views over the landscape.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Mondim de Basto?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Mondim de Basto is generally spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and more rainfall, particularly in higher elevations like the Alvão Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls in the area?
A: While not directly on every touring cycling route, the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/729193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisgas de Ermelo Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a significant natural attraction in the vicinity of Mondim de Basto and can be incorporated into a cycling trip or visited separately. Some routes might offer views or access points to areas near the waterfalls.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mondim de Basto?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling in Mondim de Basto, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the natural park landscapes, the scenic greenways, and the panoramic viewpoints that offer rewarding views after climbs.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several moderate touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32157420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Climb or Descent Pathway – Arco de Baúlhe Train Station loop from Celorico de Basto&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.9 miles (32.0 km) route that can be completed in about 2 hours.
tips: Mondim de Basto, on the banks of the Tâmega, holds memories of many peoples. Although it was only founded in the 12th century, during the reign of D. Sancho I, its roots are much older.
The trail follows the Cabril River and passes the famous swings of Vilar de Viando. The stone floor is more demanding on the legs, but the peaceful surroundings make it all worthwhile.

waypoints.name: Municipal Square of Mondim de Basto
